title: Orchis × hybrida
intro: Orchis × hybrida, also known as the hybrid orchid, is a hybrid species of flowering plant within the Orchidaceae family, resulting from the cross-breeding of two different orchid species. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Orchis × hybrida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Orchis × hybrida is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as syrups and ointments.
taxonomy: Orchis × hybrida is a hybrid of the orchid genera Orchis and possibly another genus. It is a member of the family Orchidaceae and is commonly known as the hybrid bee orchid. As a hybrid, its classification is often uncertain and may vary depending on the source. The Orchis × hybrida is often classified as a nothospecies, a hybrid species that does not exist in nature in a pure form. In botanical nomenclature, its classification is subject to change as new information becomes available.
uses: Orchis × hybrida, also known as the hybrid orchid, has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant's roots are said to contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, the roots are used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's effectiveness and safety for medicinal use. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using Orchis × hybrida as a treatment.

preparations: Orchis × hybrida, commonly known as the hybrid lady's slipper orchid, has been used in traditional herbal preparations for its potential medicinal properties.
